name: Shellcode Stub Wrapper
description: CONSTRUCT: wrap a raw shellcode blob into a runnable native PoC — a tiny C loader (mmap RW → memcpy → mprotect RX → call as int(void)) compiled with clang, whose exit code is the shellcode's return value. --emit c dumps just the loader source. The last link in the write chain: asm-assemble → shellcode-stub → exec-observe (native) or qiling-emulate (cross-arch). Builds the exe; does NOT run it.

safety.notes: CONSTRUCT skill — it BUILDS a PoC executable around the shellcode; it never runs it. The produced binary runs the shellcode when YOU run it — that's the dynamic tier (exec-observe, consent-gated) or a contained emulator (qiling-emulate). `--emit exe` needs clang (checked at runtime, honest error if absent); `--emit c` needs nothing but python3. POSIX loader (Linux/macOS).

args.name: --hex
args.desc: shellcode as a hex string instead of a file

args.name: --emit
args.desc: exe (compiled PoC, needs clang) | c (loader source only)

args.name: --target
args.desc: cross-compile triple — needs a cross sysroot (see --cflags)

args.name: --cflags
args.desc: extra clang flags (e.g. --sysroot=… -fuse-ld=lld for cross builds)

payload.note: BYO clang for --emit exe (or --emit c and hand the source to cc-build). Completes the CONSTRUCT chain: asm-assemble --out sc.bin → shellcode-stub sc.bin --out poc → exec-observe/qiling-emulate. The loader exit code is the shellcode's return value, so a `mov w0,#42; ret` PoC exits 42 — easy to assert (verified end-to-end). Native host-arch build is the guaranteed path; a cross-OS --target needs a cross sysroot/toolchain (pass it via --cflags, e.g. --sysroot=… -fuse-ld=lld) — without one the link fails honestly.
